summary: Shipped a docs-surface conversion repair to push evaluators toward the Codeberg-first path and a real first task instead of leaving the docs in browse-only mode.
why_this_action: adoption_metrics_latest.json showed Codeberg and GitHub adoption flat across the recent window, so the highest-leverage move was improving conversion on owned surfaces instead of adding more overlapping outreach.

distribution_lane_latest.md and the audit artifacts showed multiple outreach and directory lanes already inside active measurement windows, making another same-family distribution action low-signal overlap.
reddit_monitor_latest.md and reddit_post_analysis.json indicated degraded Reddit coverage / fail-closed execution, so live social posting was not the best executable lane from this runtime.

changes.change: Added a visible docs navbar CTA: Start Here -> /docs/getting-started.
Added a docs-only conversion panel with Codeberg-first CTA, first-task guide CTA, and prompt-template CTA framed around the morning-after merge test.

verification.pre_publish_release_fidelity: failed before publish with release drift detected: public/docs/index.html
verification.deploy: bundle exec cap production deploy executed via repo Ruby path and completed successfully
live_checks: live HTML contains: Best next step
live HTML contains: Choose your first task
live HTML contains: Inspect Codeberg first

expected_effect.primary_goal: Increase docs-surface evaluator flow into Codeberg and first-run/task-selection pages.
expected_effect.measurement_hint: Watch Codeberg stars/watchers/forks and any docs-path engagement deltas before adding more same-family outreach.

notes: This run chose owned-surface conversion repair over new outreach because current distribution lanes were already saturated with overlapping measurement windows.
No separate runtime-code repair was needed beyond using the repo's Ruby path for deploy execution on this host.
